2605.03782.title: What You Think is What You See: Driving Exploration in VLM Agents via Visual-Linguistic Curiosity
2605.03782.paper_summary: To navigate partially observable visual environments, recent VLM agents increasingly internalize world modeling capabilities into their policies via explicit CoT reasoning, enabling them to mentally simulate futures before acting. However, relying solely on passive reasoning over visited states is insufficient for sparse-reward tasks, as it lacks the epistemic drive to actively uncover the ``known unknown'' required for robust generalization. We ask: Can VLM agents actively find signals that challenge and refine their internal world model through curiosity-driven exploration? In this work, we propose GLANCE, a unified framework that bridges reasoning and exploration by grounding the agent's linguistic world model into the stable visual representations of an evolving target network. Crucially, GLANCE leverages the discrepancy between linguistic prediction and visual reality as an intrinsic curiosity signal within reinforcement learning, steering the agent to actively explore areas where its internal model is uncertain. Extensive experiments across a series of agentic tasks show the effectiveness of GLANCE, and demonstrate that aligning ``what the agent thinks'' with ``what the agent sees'' is key to solving complex or sparse agentic tasks.
2605.03782.paper_summary_zh: 為了在部分可觀察的視覺環境中導航，最近的 VLM 代理越來越多地將世界建模能力內化到其政策中，通過明確的 CoT 推理，使它們能夠在行動之前進行心理模擬未來。然而，僅僅依賴於對已訪問狀態的被動推理對於稀疏獎勵任務來說是不夠的，因為它缺乏主動發現“已知未知”的認識驅動，這對於穩健的泛化是必需的。我們提出問題：VLM 代理能否通過基於好奇心的探索主動找到挑戰和完善其內部世界模型的信號？在這項工作中，我們提出了 GLANCE，一個統一框架，通過將代理的語言世界模型與不斷演變的目標網絡的穩定視覺表示相結合，架起推理與探索之間的橋樑。關鍵是，GLANCE 利用語言預測與視覺現實之間的差異作為強化學習中的內在好奇心信號，指導代理主動探索其內部模型不確定的區域。在一系列代理任務中的廣泛實驗顯示了 GLANCE 的有效性，並證明了“代理所想”的與“代理所見”的對齊是解決複雜或稀疏代理任務的關鍵。
2605.03782.author: Haoxi Li et.al.
2605.03782.authors: Haoxi Li, Qinglin Hou, Jianfei Ma, Jinxiang Lai, Tao Han, Sikai Bai, Jingcai Guo, Jie Zhang, Song Guo
